zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Rich於1500年出生在英國哈普爾巴辛斯托可時，他的父親Lord Richard John Rich30歲，母親Joan Dineley28歲。他和Elisabeth Jenkes至少有2個兒子和11個女兒。他去世於1567年6月12日在英國艾可洛福德享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可武特萊斯福德。

nameMeanings.name: Rich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a wealthy man (or perhaps in some cases an ironic nickname for a pauper), from Middle English, Old French riche ‘rich, wealthy’, a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to ancient Germanic rīc ‘power(ful)’.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rich, a pet form of any of the post-Conquest names beginning in Rich-, such as Richer and especially Richard . Compare Rick .

English: either a topographic name from Middle English riche(Old English ric) ‘stream’, signifying one who lived beside a stream, as at Glynde Reach (Sussex), or perhaps a habitational name from the (now lost) village of Riche (Lincolnshire).

variants.name: Ritchie
meanings: Scottish: from a Scottish and northern English pet form of the personal name   Rich  , a short form of   Richard  . This surname is also common in Ulster.

variants.name: Ritch
meanings: English and Scottish (Orkney): either a variant of Rich , or perhaps from a short form of Ritchie , itself a diminutive based on Rich.

Altered form of German Ritsch, probably from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on rīc ‘power(ful)’ or hrōd ‘fame, renown’; or an altered form of Swiss German Rütsch, Ruetsch, from Alemannic short forms of Rudolf .

variants.name: Riches
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): variant of Rich , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Richer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Laurent, Marcel, Aurele, Donat, Elphege, Emile, Flore, Gilles.

English (East Anglia), French, and German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rich(i)er, Ricier, but was largely absorbed by the much more common Richard .

Altered form of French Héricher: from the ancient Germanic personal name Harigari, composed of the elements hari ‘army’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’, or a variant of Hérissé, a nickname for someone with spiky hair, from the adjective hérissé, a derivative of the verb se hérisser ‘to stand on end’.

variants.name: Riche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Ghislaine, Philippe, Yanick, Yvon.
French: nickname for a rich man or perhaps an ironical name for a pauper, from Old French riche ‘rich’. Compare Leriche .

variants.name: Richins
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ricun, a diminutive of Rich , itself a pet form of Richard , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. It appears that a dialectally different (Norman or Picard) form of the French name, with medial /-k-/, also survived in England for a time alongside forms with original /-tʃ-/.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Rich was born in 1500, in Basingstoke, Hampshire, England, his father, Lord Richard John Rich, was 30 and his mother, Joan Dineley, was 28. He had at least 2 sons and 11 daughters with Elisabeth Jenkes. He died on 12 June 1567, in Rochford, Essex, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Uttlesford, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Rich est né en 1500, à Basingstoke, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Richard John Rich, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Joan Dineley, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 11 filles avec Elisabeth Jenkes. Il est décédé le 12 juin 1567, à Rochford, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Uttlesford, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Richが1500年にイギリスハンプシュアイアバシングストクで生まれた時，父Lord Richard John Richは30歳，母Joan Dineleyは28歳であった。彼はElisabeth Jenkesとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と11人の娘をもうけた。彼は1567年6月12日にイギリスエセックスロチフォードで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスウットレスフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Rich(이)가 1500년 영국 함프실르 바징스토크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Richard John Rich(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Dineley(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Elisabeth Jenkes(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1567년 6월 12일 영국 에세스 록h퍼드에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 우틀레스퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Rich родился в 1500 г. в местности басингсток, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord Richard John Rich было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Dineley, 28. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 11 дочерей от Elisabeth Jenkes. Он умер 12 июня 1567 г. в местности рочфорд, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности уттлесфорд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
